free_text: (in German)
Differential cross-section of oxygen-16 for neutrons
in the MeV energy region
1 MeV Cockroft-Walton accelerator.
Deuterons incident on a heavy ice target.
Energy spread
.Chamber filling gas also served as sample.
Measurement of the energy distribution of
recoil oxygen nuclei using an ionization chamber.
Parallel plate ionization chamber filled with
99.98% pure oxygen to a pressure of 2 atm
Counting volume of height 20 mm, diam. 40 mm
field strength 4 kV/cm.
Hornyak detectors for neutron monitoring and
flux measurement.
For incident neutron intensity.
- D(d,n)
- O-16(n,tot)
.Corrections applied for variations of neutron yield
from the target during a measurement, background due
to inscattering of neutrons from the environment,
edge-effect and saturation deficit of the recoil
nuclei within the ionization chamber, variations of
the channel width of the pulse height analyzer,
contribution of the O-16(n,alpha)C-13 reaction.
Only statistical errors are given.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
REACTION code corrected in Subents 002,003. Meaningless
zeros were deleted from the data. BIB update.
INC-SPECT was added.
From NEUDADA, originally from graphs in the
publication.
SD: SF4=O-0 was added to REACTION code.
COS-CM -> COS (in the article data are given in lab.s)
EN-RSL -> EN-RSL-HW.
.Least squares fit using the computer program of
Derado and Van de Walle, CERN report 60-21 (1960).
- computer program
Table on p.364 from HPA,35,351,1962.
SD: SF4=O-0 was added to REACTION code.
NUMBER-CM -> NUMBER (in the article data are given
in the lab.system). EN-RSL -> EN-RSL-HW.
